parsed_review.summary: CP00-557 is a descriptor-only RP18 (AI Legal Workflows) closeout pack covering 40 units (RP18.P02.M04.S21-RP18.P02.M05.S30): the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (10 rows) and the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (30 rows). The pack baseline (risk B, 40 units, deliverable distribution {test:6,fixture:2,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:3,implementation:19,contract:1,security_audit:2,ui:3,failure_recovery:2}, micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:10,Permission And Audit Binding:30}) is consistent across the plan baseline, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the test, the contract, and the manifest. service.js generates only frozen descriptor objects from static constants with comprehensive no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data attestations (writes_product_state:false, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, writes_audit_event:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, creates/updates_database_rows:false, reads/writes_object_storage:false); there is no runtime execution. Claude is held as a read-only reviewer and explicitly not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false); the review packet is read_only with tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob and a forbidden-evidence blocklist (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic control-plane review packet/receipt metadata, not product runtime. The manifest and command-evidence honestly report production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review; the validator deep-equals all contract projections and command-evidence shows all gate commands (npm test, npm run validate, node --test, git diff --check) at exit 0. Readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON and validation-report confirms read-only tooling. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one non-blocking P3 informational note. Note: per the stated authority boundary, Claude is an independent read-only reviewer and not the final approval authority; enterprise-trust/runtime opening requires human final approval outside this receipt.

findings.title: Coverage summary unit_titles deduplicates across sections, so its length (30) differs from unit_count (40)
findings.details: createAiLegalWorkflowsCoreCp557CoverageSummary sets unit_titles to [...new Set(units.map(u => u.title))]. Because the M04 tail (S21-S30) and M05 (S21-S30) share ten identical row titles (e.g. 'Unit test: review path', 'Integration smoke case', 'Closeout handoff'), the de-duplicated set has 30 entries while unit_count is 40. The field is purely informational, surfaced inside the Hermes coverage summary, and is not consumed by any coverage assertion, gate, or contract deepEqual, so there is no functional or boundary impact. It is only a latent readability/foot-gun risk if a future consumer were to treat unit_titles.length as a unit count.
findings.recommendation: Optional: if a per-unit title list is ever needed downstream, key it by unit id (or use a non-deduplicated array) so length tracks unit_count; otherwise leave as-is since it is unused. No change required for CP00-557 closeout.
